title: execute_ask_user_question is a single function with cyclomatic complexity > 15
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/tools/ask_user.rs:14-130 (execute_ask_user_question)
## Violation
A single function performs argument extraction, schema validation across 7 distinct constraint families (existence of `questions`, count bounds, duplicate question texts, header presence/length, multiSelect type, options count bounds, duplicate labels, description presence, preview type) and normalization. Counted branch points include 4 `let-else`, 1 explicit `if`, 1 `for` over questions, 2 nested `for` over options, 4 `if !x.is_*` checks, and 3 `if let Some` arms. Cyclomatic complexity exceeds the framework's V(G) > 15 reject threshold. Cognitive complexity is high: rejection logic is interleaved with normalization rather than separated.

The single big function also means callers cannot validate questions independently of the JSON tool wrapper — any future consumer (`mcp.rs`, `acp.rs`, a CLI) must reimplement validation.
## Mandated refactor
Extract:
- `parse_question(value: &Value) -> Result<Question, String>` that returns a typed `Question` struct.
- `validate_question_set(qs: &[Question]) -> Result<(), String>` for cross-question uniqueness.
- `normalize_question(q: Question) -> Question` for the multi_select → multiSelect rename.
- A `Question` newtype with constructor that enforces invariants at the type boundary.

The tool entry point then becomes a 10-line orchestrator over typed values.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
